title: cyperus bellus
intro: Cyperus bellus, also known as the purple papyrus or umbrella papyrus, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family that is native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cyperus bellus include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections, as well as its potential use in treating conditions such as diabetes and hypertension. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be applied topically or ingested to reap the plant's medicinal benefits.
